title: The 8 Best Herbal Remedies to Fight Bad Breath and Halitosis

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Cineole is good for bad breath because it has antibacterial properties that help kill the germs in the mouth that cause bad breath.
Polyphenols are good for bad breath because they have antimicrobial properties that help kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath and gum disease.
Tannins are good for bad breath because they have antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can cause bad breath.
Quercetin is good for bad breath because it has natural antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams can be good for bad breath because it has antibacterial properties. This means it can kill the bacteria that cause bad breath. When you eat or drink, tiny pieces of food can stay in your mouth and create an ideal environment for bacteria to grow. These bacteria then break down the food particles and release smelly gases, causing bad breath. Eucalyptus globulus creams can help to kill these bacteria and prevent them from growing. It can also help to reduce inflammation in the mouth and gums, which can also contribute to bad breath. By using Eucalyptus globulus creams, you can help to keep your mouth clean and fresh, reducing the risk of bad breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with bad breath, you'll need to start by gathering dried Salvia officinalis leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and essential oils like peppermint or tea tree oil. Begin by heating the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the coconut oil has melted, add the dried Salvia officinalis leaves and let them steep for about 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil. After straining the oil and discarding the leaves, add the beeswax and vitamin E oil to the melted coconut oil mixture. Stir well until the beeswax has fully dissolved, then remove the mixture from the heat. Allow the mixture to cool and thicken, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a creamy consistency. Finally, add a few drops of peppermint or tea tree oil to give the cream a refreshing scent and antibacterial properties that can help combat bad breath. Pour the cream into a clean container and store it in the fridge for up to a week, applying it to the gums and teeth as needed to help freshen breath and promote oral health.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, is a natural remedy for bad breath. The creams or ointments made from peppermint oil help to freshen the breath and kill bacteria in the mouth. This is because peppermint has antibacterial properties, which fight against the germs that cause bad breath. When you apply peppermint cream to your gums, it helps to reduce inflammation and kills the bacteria that cause bad breath. The cooling sensation of peppermint also helps to numb the area, making it easier to breathe and feel fresh. Additionally, peppermint stimulates saliva production, which helps to wash away bacteria and food particles in the mouth, keeping your breath fresh for a longer period. By reducing inflammation, killing bacteria, and stimulating saliva production, Mentha x piperita creams can help to eliminate bad breath and leave your mouth feeling clean and fresh.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream to help with bad breath, start by gathering the necessary ingredients and equipment. You'll need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, coconut oil, beeswax, and a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Begin by melting the coconut oil and beeswax together in the double boiler, stirring occasionally until they're fully incorporated. Next, add a tablespoon of dried Salvia officinalis leaves to the melted mixture and let it steep for 10-15 minutes, allowing the leaves to release their active compounds into the oil. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Let the mixture cool and thicken before transferring it to a clean container. Once it's set, your Salvia officinalis herbal cream is ready to use - simply apply a small amount to your gums after brushing your teeth to help reduce bad breath and leave your mouth feeling fresh and clean.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to fight bad breath. The active compound in ginger, called gingerol, has natural antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. When applied as a cream to the mouth, gingerol works to reduce the amount of bacteria in the gums and tongue, making it a good treatment for bad breath. The cream also helps to reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can contribute to bad breath. Additionally, ginger has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe mouth sores and reduce swelling in the gums. This can help to improve the overall health of the mouth and reduce the likelihood of bad breath. When used regularly, Zingiber officinale cream can help to keep the mouth clean and fresh, making it a good addition to any oral care routine.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream for bad breath, you'll need to start by drying the Salvia leaves. This can be done by tying the leaves in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once the leaves are completely dry, you can crush them into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, combine the powdered Salvia with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the Salvia's properties. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. Once the mixture has cooled, you can add a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, to create a smooth and spreadable cream. Stir the mixture well and pour it into a clean container. Let the cream cool and solidify before using it as a mouthwash or applying it to the gums to help freshen breath and reduce bad breath.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum zeylanicum cream, also known as cinnamon oil, is good for bad breath because of its antiseptic properties. When you swallow air or eat certain foods, bacteria can grow in your mouth. These bacteria cause bad breath and can also lead to gum disease and tooth decay. The cinnamon oil in Cinnamomum zeylanicum cream helps to kill these bacteria, reducing the bad smell in your mouth. It also helps to reduce inflammation in the gums, which can make your mouth feel better and look healthier. When you apply the cream to your tongue or gums, the cinnamon oil is quickly absorbed and starts working to kill the bacteria and freshen your breath. This can help to improve the taste of your food and make you feel more confident when you talk or smile.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream for bad breath, start by drying and preparing the Salvia leaves. Harvest fresh leaves from the plant, then tie them in small bunches and hang them upside down in a warm, dry place until they are completely dry. Next, combine the dried leaves with a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Let the mixture infuse in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, and discard the solids. Now, to make the cream, mix the infused oil with a natural thickening agent like beeswax or shea butter, and a moisturizing ingredient like aloe vera or coconut oil. Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring constantly, until the beeswax or shea butter is fully melted and the mixture is smooth. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it thickens and forms a creamy consistency. Transfer the cream to a clean glass jar and store it in a cool, dry place. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to your gums or teeth after brushing your teeth, and enjoy the fresh, clean breath that Salvia officinalis provides.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum zeylanicum herbal creams for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus cream is a natural remedy that can help with bad breath. This is because it contains the essential oil of lemongrass, which has antibacterial properties. When we eat, tiny pieces of food can get stuck in our mouths and between our teeth. Bacteria on these food particles can cause bad breath. The antibacterial properties in lemongrass oil kill these bacteria, helping to prevent bad breath. Additionally, lemongrass oil has a fresh and citrusy scent that can leave your mouth feeling clean and smelling good. Using Cymbopogon citratus cream can also help to reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can help to prevent bad breath from getting worse.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with bad breath, you'll need to first dry the leaves of the sage plant, either by air-drying them or using a food dehydrator. Once the leaves are completely dry, you'll need to make a sage infusion by steeping them in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, for several weeks. The general rule of thumb is to use one part dried sage leaves to five parts carrier oil. Place the dried sage leaves in a clean glass jar and cover them with the carrier oil, then let it sit in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. This infused oil can then be mixed with a cream base, such as beeswax and coconut oil, to create a smooth and effective herbal cream that can be applied directly to the mouth or gums to help freshen breath and soothe mouth sores.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal creams for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contain thyme oil, which has been used for centuries to help freshen breath. Thyme oil has antibacterial properties, meaning it can kill the bad bacteria in your mouth that cause bad breath. When you apply the cream to your mouth or gums, it helps to reduce the amount of bacteria that can cause bad breath. Thyme oil also has a strong, pleasant scent that can help to mask any unpleasant odors in your mouth. This can give you a sense of confidence and help you feel more comfortable in social situations. The antibacterial properties of thyme oil can also help to prevent gum disease and reduce inflammation in the mouth, which can further contribute to bad breath.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Salvia officinalis herbal cream that helps with bad breath, start by gathering the necessary ingredients, including dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a preservative like vitamin E oil. Begin by infusing the dried Salvia leaves in the carrier oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof glass bowl set over a pot of simmering water, allowing the mixture to steep for 2-3 hours or overnight. Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in a separate saucepan over low heat, then slowly add the infused oil to the melted wax, stirring constantly to prevent the mixture from seizing. Continue stirring until the mixture has cooled and thickened into a smooth, creamy consistency. Once the cream has cooled, add a few drops of vitamin E oil as a preservative and mix well. Transfer the cream to a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator to prevent spoilage. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to your gums and massage gently before brushing your teeth to help freshen your breath.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia cream, also known as tea tree oil, is a natural remedy for bad breath. The cream contains compounds that kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. This is because the bacteria feed on food particles and multiply, leading to a strong unpleasant smell. The tea tree oil in the cream works to eliminate these bacteria, reducing their numbers and stopping them from producing the bad smell. Additionally, the cream's antibacterial properties help to prevent the formation of plaque, which can also contribute to bad breath. By killing bacteria and preventing plaque formation, the cream can help to freshen the breath and improve oral health.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal cream for bad breath, start by gathering the necessary ingredients and equipment. You will need dried Salvia officinalis leaves, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. First, infuse the dried Salvia leaves in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in the double boiler and slowly add the infused oil, stirring constantly to combine. Continue heating until the mixture reaches a smooth, creamy consistency. Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool slightly before transferring it to a clean container. Once the cream has cooled and thickened, it is ready to use as a natural remedy for bad breath. Simply apply a small amount to your gums or teeth as needed to help freshen and protect your mouth.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for bad breath

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, is a plant with many health benefits. One of its uses is for bad breath. This is because sage contains compounds that have antibacterial properties, which help kill the bacteria in the mouth that can cause bad breath. The antibacterial properties in sage can reduce the amount of bad breath-causing bacteria, like Streptococcus and Escherichia.  When you apply Salvia officinalis cream to your mouth, the antibacterial compounds can reach the areas where the bad breath bacteria are. This can help stop the bacteria from growing and causing bad breath. The cream also contains flavonoids, which are known to reduce inflammation in the mouth. This can help soothe mouth sores and gum inflammation, which can be contributing factors to bad breath. The flavonoids in Salvia officinalis cream may also help reduce the odor-causing compounds in the mouth. When you eat, bacteria in the mouth break down food particles and release volatile sulfur compounds, which are responsible for bad breath. The flavonoids in sage cream may help reduce the amount of these compounds, which can help make your breath smell fresher. In traditional medicine, Salvia officinalis has been used for centuries to treat mouth and gum problems. This includes sore throats, gum inflammation, and bad breath. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of sage make it a popular ingredient in natural mouthwashes and creams. By applying Salvia officinalis cream to your mouth, you may be able to reduce bad breath and other mouth problems.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Salvia officinalis herbal creams that help with bad breath, you will need fresh or dried sage leaves, a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and essential oils like peppermint or tea tree oil. First, prepare the sage leaves by cleaning and drying them. If you are using fresh leaves, dry them in a low-temperature oven or by tying them in a bunch and hanging them upside down. Next, mix 1/4 cup of dried sage leaves with 1 cup of carrier oil in a saucepan, and heat the mixture over low heat for a few hours to create a strong infusion. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids, then discard the solids. In a separate saucepan, melt 2 tablespoons of beeswax and add 1 tablespoon of the sage-infused oil, stirring until the wax is fully incorporated. Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in a few drops of peppermint or tea tree essential oil for its fresh and antibacterial properties. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify. Once set, your Salvia officinalis herbal cream is ready to use as a mouthwash or a breath freshener, applied directly to the gums or tongue to help reduce bad breath.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal creams for bad breath

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be an effective solution for bad breath. Eucalyptus globulus, an herb commonly found in gum and mouthwash products, has antibacterial properties that help kill the bacteria in your mouth responsible for bad breath. Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, has a refreshing and cooling effect that can help reduce inflammation in the mouth and throat, which can contribute to bad breath. When used in a cream form, these herbs can be applied directly to the affected area, providing quick and targeted relief.  Bad breath, also known as halitosis, can be a significant issue affecting a person's self-confidence and overall well-being. It's not just a matter of personal hygiene, but also a sign of an underlying health issue, such as gum disease, diabetes, or sinus infections. Fixing bad breath is essential to improve your quality of life, as it can help you feel more confident and comfortable in social situations, which can lead to improved relationships and better mental health. By using herbal creams containing Eucalyptus globulus and Mentha x piperita, you can take a proactive approach to addressing this issue and start feeling more confident and carefree.
supplementary_best: A combination of herbal creams to remedy bad breath can be created using Eucalyptus globulus, Thymus vulgaris, and Melaleuca alternifolia. Eucalyptus globulus has antibacterial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which is often the cause of bad breath. Thymus vulgaris has a strong antimicrobial effect, killing bacteria and other microorganisms that can lead to bad breath. Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, has antiseptic properties that help prevent infections in the mouth. When combined, these three herbs work synergistically to effectively reduce the bacteria that cause bad breath and prevent infections in the mouth. This combination is particularly effective because it targets both the cause and the prevention of bad breath, making it a powerful remedy for this common issue.
supplementary_avoid: If you have bad breath, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One of these is 'Lavandula angustifolia', commonly known as lavender. While lavender has a calming effect, its oil can cause mouth dryness, which can make bad breath worse.  Another herbal cream to avoid is 'Rosmarinus officinalis', or rosemary. Its oil has a strong flavor that can make bad breath worse. Rosemary can also cause mouth irritation, which can lead to bad breath.  'Sassafras albidum', or sassafras, is another herbal cream to avoid. Sassafras oil has a strong flavor that can make bad breath worse. It can also cause mouth irritation, which can lead to bad breath.  'Asafoetida', or Ferula asafoetida, is a herbal cream to avoid if you have bad breath. Its oil has a strong, unpleasant flavor that can make bad breath worse. Asafoetida can also cause mouth irritation, which can lead to bad breath.  'Foeniculum vulgare', or fennel, is another herbal cream to avoid. Its oil can cause mouth dryness, which can make bad breath worse. Fennel can also cause mouth irritation, which can lead to bad breath.  'Glycyrrhiza glabra', or licorice root, is a herbal cream to avoid if you have bad breath. Its oil can cause mouth dryness, which can make bad breath worse. Licorice root can also cause mouth irritation, which can lead to bad breath.
supplementary_other_remedies: Bad breath, also known as halitosis, can be caused by poor oral hygiene, gum disease, or eating certain foods. Besides herbal creams, there are other natural remedies that can help. One of these remedies is oil pulling with coconut oil or sesame oil. Oil pulling involves swishing oil around in the mouth to remove bacteria and germs that cause bad breath. The oil helps to loosen and pull out these bacteria, leaving the mouth feeling fresh and clean.  Another remedy is chewing parsley or dill. These herbs have natural antibacterial properties that help to kill the bacteria in the mouth that cause bad breath. Chewing on them after meals can help to reduce bad breath. Some people also drink apple cider vinegar as a natural remedy for bad breath. The acidity in the vinegar helps to kill bacteria in the mouth and balance the pH levels, which can help to reduce bad breath.  In addition to these remedies, some people find that gargling with salt water helps to reduce bad breath. The salt helps to kill bacteria in the mouth and reduce inflammation, which can help to reduce bad breath. Finally, some people find that drinking plenty of water and eating crunchy fruits and vegetables like apples and carrots helps to stimulate saliva production, which can help to wash away bacteria and germs that cause bad breath.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with bad breath, also known as halitosis, may also experience other health issues. One common problem is gum disease, which is an infection of the gums caused by poor oral hygiene. Medicinal herbs like neem and licorice root can help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the mouth, which can help to heal gum disease. This is because neem has antibacterial properties that can help to combat infections, while licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe and calm the gums.  Another issue people with bad breath may experience is digestive problems, such as bloating and gas. This is because bad breath can be a sign of an underlying digestive issue, such as irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or small intestine bacterial overgrowth (SIBO). Medicinal herbs like peppermint and ginger can help to soothe the digestive system and reduce symptoms of bloating and gas. Peppermint has a calming effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, while ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system.  People with bad breath may also experience skin problems, such as acne and eczema. This is because the same bacteria that cause bad breath can also cause skin infections. Medicinal herbs like turmeric and aloe vera can help to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin. Turmeric has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and swelling, while aloe vera has soothing properties that can help to calm and cool the skin.  In addition, people with bad breath may experience respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and sinusitis. This is because the same bacteria that cause bad breath can also cause respiratory infections. Medicinal herbs like eucalyptus and thyme can help to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria in the respiratory tract. Eucalyptus has decongestant properties that can help to relieve congestion, while thyme has antibacterial properties that can help to combat infections.  Finally, people with bad breath may experience thyroid problems, such as hypothyroidism. This is because poor oral health can lead to an imbalance of thyroid hormones. Medicinal herbs like ashwagandha and bladderwrack can help to support thyroid function and reduce symptoms of hypothyroidism. Ashwagandha has adaptogenic properties that can help to balance hormones, while bladderwrack has thyroid-stimulating properties that can help to increase thyroid hormone production.
